$comment: Register semantics for the PumpSaver Plus 233-P IR broadcast (spec v0.2). confidence: verified = cross-checked against external ground truth or arithmetic structure; candidate = consistent hypothesis (strong = rivals refuted); unknown = observed value only. Observed values are from a 233-P on a ~240V well pump, Nov-Dec 2025.

protocol.word: 0x90 | reg:8 | value:16 BE, MSB-first, trailing zero-bits omitted
protocol.ring_block: 0x19-0x75, round-robined in 4 chunks, full refresh every ~5.8 s
protocol.mcu: Microchip PIC16F684 (bit-banged IR, no UART, 256B EEPROM holds ring+cal)

registers.name: pump_starts
registers.notes: Lifetime pump-start counter (user-clearable). +1 within ~2 s of every ground-truth pump start; strictly monotone across 24 days of captures.

registers.name: power
registers.notes: TRUE active power, no correction needed. ~26 W idle (device self-draw), ~820 W running, inrush peaks ~1700. P = V*I_true*PF holds at both operating points.

registers.name: voltage
registers.notes: Live line voltage x10. Tracks sags from any load on the leg, not just the pump. Observed lifetime range 233.1-248.8 V.

registers.name: current
registers.notes: LEG-SUM channel: reads ~2x true motor current while the pump runs (both 240V hot legs pass the sensing path; true running amps = value/200); device idle draw is single-count (value/100). Fault log stores single-count amps.

registers.name: power_factor
registers.notes: ~0.78 idle and running; consistent with P/(V*I) at both operating points; heavily averaged (unchanged at inrush samples).

registers.name: run_minutes
registers.notes: Cumulative run-time in minutes (user-clearable). +1 per 60 s of running; tick carry-chains were the arithmetic proof of the encoding.

registers.name: calibration_power
registers.notes: Calibration power (953 W). Running load = 86% of it; all 19 fault-log wattages inside the documented 70-90% dry-well band. Refuted as trip threshold and as any amps record.

registers.name: calibration_voltage
registers.notes: 238.4 V, within 2% of modal live V. Refuted as max-V record.

registers.name: overcurrent_trip
registers.notes: 1223 = exactly 125% x implied cal current (leg-sum scale; ~4.9 A true cal current). Refuted as min/max-amps record.

registers.name: low_voltage_trip_or_min
registers.notes: 211.2 V. Low-voltage trip OR min-volts-since-cal; 24 days of data never discriminated.

registers.name: const_0x07
registers.notes: 45060 = 0xB004 = 751 h x 60 exactly, or an ID/bit-field. Unresolved.

registers.name: oc_trip_delay
registers.notes: 50 = 5.0 s — matches the manual's fixed 5 s overcurrent trip delay.

registers.name: high_voltage_trip_or_max
registers.notes: 248.8 V. High-voltage trip OR max-volts-since-cal (live V once grazed exactly 2488 for one sample).

registers.name: firmware_version
registers.notes: 1027 = 0x0403 — version 4.03? Refuted as an amps record.

registers.name: min_or_nominal_voltage
registers.notes: 231.5 V. Min-volts-since-cal or nominal voltage; refuted as max-V record.

registers.name: drywell_trip_delay
registers.notes: 41 = 4.1 s ≈ the manual's fixed 4 s dry-well trip delay.

blocks.notes: Observed constant 0. Restart-delay setting/remaining and CT size are expected here (all 0 / n-a except during a lockout or on CT-equipped models).
20 x 4-bit fault codes, packed MSB-first, NEWEST FIRST. Observed [4, 1 x19]. Code 1 = dry-well (proven via 4-s retry spacing); code 4 unidentified.

19 x (W, V x10, A x100) fault snapshots on a rigid 3-register grid: record k starts at 0x1E+3k, newest first. Amps are single-count. Observed: W 755-838, V 2394-2432, A 541-660 (implied PF 0.52-0.57 = underload signature).
20 x 3-byte fault timestamps: 24-bit BE, run-clock MINUTES (same unit as 0x17), newest first. Observed [32572, 6703 x14, 6702 x5] — a 19-trip dry-well retry storm plus one code-4 event.
Trailer word 0x7E3C (= code-4 timestamp - 256 min exactly). Never updated at a normal pump start. Unresolved.
